data.narrative:
ispace is a public-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Takeshi Hakamada that has raised $200M+. The valuation analysis relies on a peer-median approach against four same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

The peer-median method yields an implied valuation of $6.7B, representing the midpoint across the four public comparables in the Space & Aerospace sector. The peer set spans a wide range from $900M to $37.0B, reflecting material dispersion in how the public markets currently value lunar and space infrastructure operators at similar maturity levels. A public-comp anchoring approach using Price-to-Sales multiples (sector median 59.8x across six public tickers) cannot be applied without disclosure of ispace's revenue.

The triangulated valuation range is $5.7B to $7.6B, with a median point of $6.7B. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the wide valuation dispersion within the peer set—a spread of 41x between Astroscale and Rocket Lab—and the absence of revenue-based cross-checks. Investors should weight the median estimate against their own views on ispace's mission cadence, technology differentiation, and commercial traction relative to the four reference companies.
